raw_materials.name: Quartz (SiO2)
raw_materials.notes: High-purity quartz needed for crucibles and electronics

raw_materials.name: Iron ore (hematite, magnetite)
raw_materials.notes: Most-used metal throughout the entire sequence

raw_materials.name: Copper ore (malachite, chalcopyrite)
raw_materials.notes: Electrical applications make copper indispensable

raw_materials.name: Carbon (charcoal/coke)
raw_materials.notes: Essential reducing agent for metallurgy and silicon production

raw_materials.name: Wool
raw_materials.notes: Warmth and insulation; fire-resistant when treated

raw_materials.name: Timber (mine shoring)
raw_materials.notes: Structural support for underground mines; straight-grained hardwoods preferred

raw_materials.name: Flax
raw_materials.notes: Primary fiber crop for temperate climates; retting yields long fibers

raw_materials.name: Hemp
raw_materials.notes: Extremely versatile fiber; also provides seed oil

raw_materials.name: Oil seeds (rapeseed, castor, linseed)
raw_materials.notes: Source of vegetable oils for lubrication and coatings

raw_materials.name: Animal fats (tallow, lard)
raw_materials.notes: First lubricant source; tallow for heavy bearings, lard for light mechanisms

raw_materials.name: Petroleum (crude oil)
raw_materials.notes: Massive accelerator if available; not required if absent

raw_materials.name: Coal tar
raw_materials.notes: Historical source of organic chemicals before petroleum

raw_materials.name: Natural rubber (latex)
raw_materials.source: Rubber tree (Hevea brasiliensis) tapping or guayule shrub
raw_materials.notes: Essential for gaskets, seals, and flexible components; guayule is a temperate-climate alternative

raw_materials.name: Sulfur
raw_materials.source: Volcanic deposits / Frasch process / smelter offgas
raw_materials.notes: Dual-use: vulcanizing agent for rubber and feedstock for sulfuric acid

raw_materials.name: Glass fiber raw materials (silica sand, limestone, borax)
raw_materials.notes: Borosilicate glass fiber for structural composites; borax may be a bottleneck

raw_materials.name: Copper wire stock
raw_materials.notes: Winding wire for electromagnets, transformers, motors, and magnetic core memory; enameled for insulation

raw_materials.name: Type metal (lead-antimony-tin alloy)
raw_materials.notes: Movable type casting; antimony provides hardness and reduces shrinkage; tin improves fluidity for casting

raw_materials.name: Gum arabic / acacia resin
raw_materials.source: Acacia tree sap (wild harvest or cultivation)
raw_materials.notes: Binder for iron gall ink and carbon ink; controls flow and adhesion on parchment and paper

raw_materials.name: Medicinal plants (willow, cinchona, opium poppy, ephedra, foxglove)
raw_materials.notes: Primary source of analgesics (salicin), antimalarials (quinine), anesthetics (morphine), bronchodilators (ephedrine), cardiac drugs (digitalis)

raw_materials.name: Ethanol (grain alcohol)
raw_materials.notes: Solvent for drug extraction (tinctures), antiseptic (60-70%), preservative, and precursor for diethyl ether anesthetic

raw_materials.name: Gauge block steel (hardened chrome alloy / tungsten carbide)
raw_materials.notes: Fundamental length reference for precision manufacturing; must be dimensionally stable, wear-resistant, and low thermal expansion

raw_materials.name: Manganin wire (84% Cu, 12% Mn, 4% Ni)
raw_materials.notes: Resistance standard wire with near-zero temperature coefficient (~20 ppm/°C); bifilar wound on brass bobbin for standard resistors

raw_materials.name: Novolac resin precursors (phenol, formaldehyde)
raw_materials.notes: Base resin for standard positive-tone photoresist (novolac + DNQ); phenol-formaldehyde condensation polymer

raw_materials.name: Fused quartz mask blanks
raw_materials.source: High-purity silica melting / synthetic silica deposition
raw_materials.notes: Photomask substrate; must transmit i-line (365 nm) — soda-lime glass absorbs below 350 nm; polished to λ/4 flatness

raw_materials.name: Specialty etching gases (CF₄, SF₆, Cl₂, BCl₃)
raw_materials.source: Chemical synthesis from elemental feedstocks (fluorite, salt, sulfur)
raw_materials.notes: RIE and DRIE process gases; CF₄/SF₆ for silicon etching, Cl₂/BCl₃ for aluminum; potent greenhouse gases requiring abatement

raw_materials.name: ALD precursors (trimethylaluminum, TiCl₄, TDMAH)
raw_materials.source: Organometallic synthesis from aluminum/metal chlorides + methylation
raw_materials.notes: Atomic layer deposition precursors for high-k dielectrics (HfO₂), barrier layers (TiN), and gate oxides (Al₂O₃); self-limiting reactions

raw_materials.name: Seamless steel tubing and PTFE sealant
raw_materials.notes: High-pressure gas piping and chemically inert gasket/seal material; PTFE for corrosive gas service and ultra-pure gas lines

raw_materials.name: Clay minerals (kaolin, fireclay, ball clay)
raw_materials.source: Surface mining (river banks, floodplains, clay pits)
raw_materials.notes: Foundation of all ceramic production — pottery, crucibles, refractory bricks, and kiln linings; fireclay essential for furnace temperatures above 1200°C

raw_materials.name: Limestone (CaCO₃)
raw_materials.notes: Source of quicklime (CaO) for mortar, plaster, whitewash, soil amendment, hide processing, and sewage treatment; also glass batch ingredient

raw_materials.name: Rail steel (wrought iron or steel T-rails)
raw_materials.notes: Inverted-T cross-section rails (15-30 kg/m); initially iron straps on timber, progressing to rolled steel rail

raw_materials.name: Treated timber (sleepers, telegraph poles, shoring)
raw_materials.notes: Railroad sleepers, telegraph poles, mine shoring, and bridge timbers; creosote or tar treatment prevents decay

raw_materials.name: Optical glass blanks (crown glass, flint glass, borosilicate)
raw_materials.source: Glass melting and casting (from glass production)
raw_materials.notes: Lens and prism blanks for microscopes, telescopes, and spectroscopes; crown + flint glass pair corrects chromatic aberration

raw_materials.name: Polishing compounds (cerium oxide, iron oxide rouge)
raw_materials.source: Mineral processing (rare earth concentrates / hematite grinding)
raw_materials.notes: CeO₂ slurry for final optical polish (faster, cleaner); Fe₂O₃ rouge as alternative; also used for CMP in semiconductor fabrication

raw_materials.name: Seeds and planting stock (staple crops, medicinal herbs, fiber plants)
raw_materials.source: Wild collection / seed saving / selective breeding
raw_materials.notes: Foundation of agricultural civilization — grain staples, legumes, medicinal species, fiber crops (flax, hemp), and dye plants; seed viability declines over years without replanting

bottleneck_materials.reason: Required for fused silica crucibles; natural deposits of sufficient purity are rare
bottleneck_materials.mitigation: Zone refining or chemical purification of available quartz

bottleneck_materials.reason: Source of HF for silicon etching; may not be locally available
bottleneck_materials.mitigation: Alternative etching chemistries (KOH, TMAH) for some steps; fluorite trade if possible

bottleneck_materials.reason: Wire production for generators, motors, and distribution requires tons of copper
bottleneck_materials.mitigation: Aluminum wire as substitute where acceptable; aggressive recycling

bottleneck_materials.reason: Required for contact process and other catalytic reactions
bottleneck_materials.mitigation: Lead chamber process as fallback; alternative catalysts research
